risks.description: Exposure to diverse operational challenges due to global business complexity.
Impact from commercial factors across markets and evolving industry dynamics.
Financial and strategic risks arising from broader economic conditions.
Potential challenges from evolving regulations affecting business operations.
Proactive identification, assessment, and mitigation of risks (short- to medium-term & long-term).
mitigation: Structured approach: identifying, assessing, mitigating, monitoring risks.
Transparency & shared understanding via ERM governance.

risks.description: Material sustainability topics like climate change mitigation and responsible business conduct.
mitigation: Reviewed every six months by Executive Management, Board, and Audit Committee.

risks.description: Conflicts, trade barriers and assertive industrial policies create supply chain disruptions and market volatility.
Industrial policies and evolving standards challenge compliance and market access.

risks.risk_title: Project execution delays due to scaling complexity
risks.description: Offshore project scale and quality/cost/timeline risks lead to delivery delays and reputational damage.
Evolving cyber-attack methods pose dual risks to Vestas and its customers, threatening critical assets.
GHG emissions from operations and value chain pose climate impacts and financial implications; low-emission materials present opportunities.
mitigation: Offering low-emission materials improves value proposition

risks.description: Increased carbon taxes and tariffs raise costs for GHG-intensive materials like steel, impacting turbine production costs.
Fossil energy use in manufacturing and service of turbines negatively impacts climate; scored as immaterial for financial materiality.
Negative biodiversity and ecosystem impacts from GHG emissions across extraction, production, and transport.
Landfill or incineration of non-recyclable blade materials increases environmental impact.
mitigation: Offering blade circularity solutions improves value proposition

risks.risk_title: Resource inflow risks in upstream supply chain
risks.description: Climate and environmental impacts from extraction and production of metals and raw materials.
Actual health and safety incidents among employees cause delays, lost work hours, and compensation costs.
Upstream workers' health and safety incidents and contractor injuries can lead to delays and compensation costs.

risks.risk_title: Forced or child labour in supply chain
risks.description: Potential forced or child labour in the value chain carries fines and reputational risks due to global supplier network.

risks.risk_title: Land-use restrictions during wind farm construction
risks.description: Temporary negative social and cultural impacts from displacement of local communities during construction.

risks.risk_title: Failure to respect indigenous peoples' land rights
risks.description: Violation of indigenous peoples' rights in wind farm development and operation.

risks.risk_title: Inadequate grid expansion and permit delays
risks.description: Political engagement ineffectiveness leading to higher costs, reduced investments, and lower wind deployment viability.

risks.risk_title: Cyber attacks on wind farm infrastructure
risks.description: Potential disruptions from cyberattacks causing power outages, IP exposure, and higher electricity prices.
Global presence raises exposure to corruption and bribery, though scored immaterial in the report.
High carbon taxes on steel and iron increase material costs for turbines, raising overall expenses.
Failure to meet Scope 1/2/3 GHG targets could lead to penalties or reputational damage.
mitigation: GHG reduction targets, transition plan for climate mitigation

risks.description: Slow adoption of low-emission materials by suppliers may force Vestas to absorb costs or shift pricing.
mitigation: Engagement with industry associations, piloting renewably fuelled services

risks.risk_title: Market transition risk from carbon tariffs
risks.description: Carbon tariffs could penalize high-emission components, reducing export opportunities or increasing costs.
Fossil fuel reliance in operations raises emissions and operational costs despite decarbonization efforts.
mitigation: Cutting emissions from vessels, transitioning to low-emission fuels

risks.description: Unforeseen political shifts could alter wind energy expansion timelines or regulatory frameworks.
Growing Scope 3 emissions may exceed targets if decarbonization levers are insufficient.
mitigation: GHG reduction targets, collaboration with suppliers

risks.risk_title: High CAPEX and OPEX for renewable transition
risks.description: Significant CAPEX/OPEX investments required to align operations with taxonomy and climate targets, creating financial strain.
Dependence on supportive policies to meet global climate targets; lack of action may hinder progress.
Scope 3 reduction target requires supplier collaboration and low-emission material procurement.
mitigation: Additional orders for low-emission steel in 2025

risks.description: Slow EV adoption and operational constraints hinder fleet electrification targets.
Pilot methanol-powered vessel introduces transition uncertainty and operational/class approval needs.
mitigation: 10-year charter agreement with Northern Offshore Service

risks.description: Dependence on RECs and supplier renewability; no locked-in assets for Scope 1/2 emissions.
Physical risks like water stress and extreme rainfall may impact operations; requires scenario-based adaptation.
Limited SAF availability constrains emissions reductions from project-related flights; pilot projects aim to expand use.

risks.risk_title: Cyber Attacks on Vestas' Digital Infrastructure
risks.description: Potential cyber incidents disrupting power production, outages, and financial losses due to politically/motivated actors targeting wind turbines and grid control systems.
mitigation: Customer cyber risk management (protecting energy solutions)
Operational cyber risk management (digital systems oversight)
Cyber Security Services Framework (quantitative risk assessment, threat intelligence, ISA/IEC 62443 compliance)
Contribution to open-source initiatives and industry resilience programs

risks.risk_title: Geopolitical Cyber Threats Affecting Vestas' Customers
risks.description: Increased risk of cyber incidents due to geopolitical instability, targeting customers/producers via Vestas’ exposure as a potential attack vector.
mitigation: Sharing expertise in international organizations (e.g., shaping cyber legislation)
Enhanced stakeholder trust through transparent risk communication
